nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Harrington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the three places called Harrington (Cumberland, Lincolnshire, Northamptonshire). The Cumberland placename derives from the Old English personal name Hæfer + Old English connective -ing- + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The Lincolnshire placename derives from the Old English personal name Hearra + Old English connective -ing- + tūn. The Northamptonshire derives from an Old English personal name Hǣthhere + Old English connective -ing- + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Herendeen .
Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hArrachtáin ‘descendant of Arrachtán’, a personal name from a diminutive of arrachtach ‘mighty, powerful’.
Irish: in Kerry, this name was adopted as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hIongardail, later Ó hUrdáil, ‘descendant of Iongardal’, a personal name of uncertain origin.

variants.name: Herington
meanings: English: variant of   Harrington   or   Herrington  .

variants.name: Herrington
meanings: habitational name from Heronden in Tenterden (Kent) or Herrington in Houghton (Durham). The Kent placename may derive from an Old English personal name Hȳra + Old English connective -ing- + denn ‘pasture’, while the Durham placename may come from Old English hæring ‘rocky place’ (from hær ‘rock, heap of stones’ + the placename forming suffix -ing) or hæren ‘rocky’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Arrington
meanings: variant of Harrington with loss of initial H-.
possibly a habitational name from Arrington, a place in Cambridgeshire, named from an Old English byname, Earn(a), meaning ‘eagle’ + -inga- ‘people or followers of’ + tūn ‘settlement’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert De Harrington was born in 1445, in England, his father, John Gage Harrington, was 25 and his mother, Catherine Culpeper, was 27. He married Mathilde Maud Prisett in 1470, in England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons. In 1498, at the age of 53, his occupation is listed as high sheriff in Rutland, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert De Harrington est né en 1445, à Angleterre, son père, John Gage Harrington, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Catherine Culpeper, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Mathilde Maud Prisett en 1470, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils. En 1498, à l’âge de 53 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est high sheriff à Rutland, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert De Harrington 1445 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Gage Harrington 25 und seine Mutter Catherine Culpeper 27. Er hat 1470 in England Mathilde Maud Prisett geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne. 1498, als er 53 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als high sheriff in Rutland, England aufgeführt.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert De Harrington è nato il nel 1445 nel England, il suo padre, John Gage Harrington, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Catherine Culpeper, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Mathilde Maud Prisett nel 1470, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli. Nel 1498, all'età di 53 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come high sheriff nel Rutland, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert De Harrington(이)가 1445년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Gage Harrington(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Catherine Culpeper(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1470년 영국에서 Mathilde Maud Prisett(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 1498년, 53세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국 루틀랜드에서 high sheriff이었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert De Harrington nasceu em 1445, em Inglaterra, seu pai, John Gage Harrington, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Catherine Culpeper, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Mathilde Maud Prisett em 1470, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos. Em 1498, com 53 anos, sua profissão aparece como high sheriff em Rutland, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert De Harrington родился в 1445 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени John Gage Harrington было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Catherine Culpeper, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Mathilde Maud Prisett в 1470 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей. В 1498 году, в возрасте 53 лет,  он работал по профессии high sheriff в местности рутланд, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert De Harrington nació en 1445, en Inglaterra, su papá, John Gage Harrington, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Catherine Culpeper, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Mathilde Maud Prisett en 1470, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos. En 1498, a la edad de 53 años, su ocupación registrada era high sheriff en Rutland, Inglaterra.
